channel.title: Science - Latest - Google News
channel.copyright: Copyright © 2026 Google. All rights reserved. This XML feed is made available solely for the purpose of rendering Google News results within a personal feed reader for personal, non-commercial use. Any other use of the feed is expressly prohibited. By accessing this feed or using these results in any manner whatsoever, you agree to be bound by the foregoing restrictions.

item.title: An asteroid discovered days ago will narrowly miss Earth - CNN
item.description:
1. An asteroid discovered days ago will narrowly miss Earth  CNN
  2. Watch an asteroid the size of a blue whale hurtle towards Earth live online TODAY  Space
  3. Near-Earth Object 2026 JH2 Ranks Zero on the Loeb Scale  Avi Loeb – Medium
  4. Asteroid 2026 JH2 Is About to Fly Right Past Earth—Relatively Speaking  WIRED
  5. This asteroid is as big as a basketball court and it's about to skim past Earth, even closer than the Moon  BBC Sky at Night Magazine

item.title: It takes a village – or a Phoenix suburb – to wrangle a wayward tortoise - NPR
item.description: It takes a village – or a Phoenix suburb – to wrangle a wayward tortoise  NPR

item.title: The James Webb Space Telescope is currently observing galaxies that formed 13 billion years ago, and several of them shouldn't exist according to the model cosmologists were using when Webb launched - Space Daily
item.description:
1. The James Webb Space Telescope is currently observing galaxies that formed 13 billion years ago, and several of them shouldn't exist according to the model cosmologists were using when Webb launched  Space Daily
  2. Supernova dust may be behind one of JWST's biggest puzzles  Phys.org
  3. Webb Telescope Reveals Brown Dwarfs Masquerading as Early Galaxies  Sky & Telescope
  4. Astronomers Stunned by Ancient Galaxy With No Spin  SciTechDaily
  5. Massive black hole from the early universe has astronomers looking back to the Big Bang  AOL.com

item.description:

item.title: NASA releases final RFP for Mars communications orbiter - SpaceNews
item.description:
1. NASA releases final RFP for Mars communications orbiter  SpaceNews
  2. NASA Draws on Industry for Mars Telecommunications Network  NASA (.gov)
  3. NASA just put a 30-day clock on a $700 million Mars contract, and the deadline tells you everything about how scared the agency is of losing its relay orbiters before astronauts arrive  Space Daily
  4. What’s up with… NASA, telco M&A, Verizon  telecomtv.com
  5. NASA Kicks Off Mars Telecom Network Competition  Aviation Week

item.title: SpaceX Starlink and other satellite megaconstellations are creating an 'unregulated geoengineering experiment', scientists say - Space
item.description:
1. SpaceX Starlink and other satellite megaconstellations are creating an 'unregulated geoengineering experiment', scientists say  Space
  2. Satellite launch pollution is becoming a major climate threat, on top of the huge space debris problem that already exists  Earth.com
  3. Satellite launches are filling up the atmosphere, study warns  The Independent
  4. A Falcon 9 upper stage burned up over Europe last February, and German scientists just caught it doing something to the atmosphere nobody had directly measured before  Space Daily
  5. We fixed the ozone crisis once. These new rockets could erode it once more  BBC Science Focus Magazine

item.title: Antarctic Sea Ice Enters 'Shock' Decline as Ocean Heat Breaks Through - ScienceAlert
item.description:
1. Antarctic Sea Ice Enters 'Shock' Decline as Ocean Heat Breaks Through  ScienceAlert
  2. The hole in the ice at the end of the Earth  The Seattle Times
  3. Scientists identify hidden accelerant in Antarctic ice loss  Phys.org
  4. Antarctica’s sudden sea ice loss is one of the most extreme and confusing events in the modern climate record. Scientists now know why it's happening.  Live Science
  5. Scientists found hidden warm-water channels under one of Antarctica’s biggest glaciers, and it changed what they feared about melting ice  The Times of India

item.title: Scientists finally solve why humans became overwhelmingly right-handed - Interesting Engineering
item.description:
1. Scientists finally solve why humans became overwhelmingly right-handed  Interesting Engineering

item.title: The Parker Solar Probe is now moving at 430,000 miles per hour, fast enough to cross the continental United States in 20 seconds, and it survives skimming the Sun's corona because a four-and-a-half-inch slab of carbon foam stays at room temperature on the - Space Daily
item.description: The Parker Solar Probe is now moving at 430,000 miles per hour, fast enough to cross the continental United States in 20 seconds, and it survives skimming the Sun's corona because a four-and-a-half-inch slab of carbon foam stays at room temperature on the  Space Daily
